title: Oracle Twists
description: Story-level reversals for solo scenes — original table.
entries: Someone you counted out returns at the worst possible moment.
The thing you were promised has already been given to someone else.
Your goal is exactly where you thought — but it isn't what you thought.
An ally asks you for the one thing you can't afford to give.
The villain of this scene is right about something important.
What looked like the destination is a waypoint. The trail continues.
Someone has been here before you, and recently.
The help you sent for arrives — owed to someone else now.
A small kindness you did earlier comes back, grown.
The rumor was wrong about the what, right about the who.
You are recognized. Not for what you expected.
The deadline just moved. Not in your favor.
Two of your problems turn out to be the same problem.
The safe route is closed. The other route is open and lit.
Someone offers to solve this for you. The price is postponed.
It was a test, and passing it is what causes the trouble.
The object changes hands in front of you.
Weather, water, or fire takes the choice out of everyone's hands.
A bystander picks a side. Loudly.
The truth would fix this, and telling it would break something else.
You find a letter meant for the next person to stand where you stand.
The opposition sends an apology — and a better offer.
What you're carrying is heavier than it was. Someone knows why.
The scene ends early: interrupted by tomorrow's problem.
